steps.title: Use tangent lengths
steps.content: The radii to points M and N are each $144$ millimeters. Tangent segments from the same external point are equal, so let each be $t$.

steps.title: Find the distance to H
steps.content:
A radius to a tangent point is perpendicular to the tangent, so triangle GMH is right.
$$d^2=144^2+640^2$$
$$d^2=430{,}336$$
$$d=656$$
The answer is D.
